description: In a 'keli', the parts of a made object or assembly are arranged to perform a particular function. 'tenoa' (tool) emphasizes what a user employs to do a task, while 'keli' draws attention to the object's own working arrangement. One object can be both when both views matter.
articulatory_notes: Raise the back of the tongue to the soft palate for 'k' and release into stressed mid-front 'e'. Touch the tongue tip to the alveolar ridge for lateral 'l' and let the air pass along the sides before high-front 'i'.
usage_notes: A device may be simple or complex. When a technical analysis matters, Systems vocabulary can name its components, inputs, outputs, interface, state, and failure conditions.

semantic_domains.creation: a made arrangement of parts with a particular function
